title: Recursive Containers, Stack Parsing, Depth, and Recovery · Behavior Case: Container Stack
summary: Deeply nested lists overflow recursion, an unmatched container consumes the rest of the file, child offsets drift, and one invalid construct prevents later valid content from rendering. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the container stack increment in bounded nested-block parser and recovery trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Use an explicit open-container stack where depth, continuation, closure, and source ownership need controlled iteration.
Define base case, recursive reduction, returned state, source advancement, and invariant for nested document structures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-container-stack — Use an explicit open-container stack where depth, continuation, closure, and source ownership need controlled iteration.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-nesting-recursion-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-nesting-recursion-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_nesting_recursion_5(containers_opened: int = 7, containers_closed: int = 7, max_depth: int = 6, recovery_points: int = 2, stalled_positions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-count"
    if containers_closed != containers_opened:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-balance"
    if max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    if recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0:
        return False, stalled_positions, "recovery-progress"
    assert containers_closed == containers_opened
    return True, max_depth, "recoverable-nesting"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-container-stack-1mrslb
# Competency: ssg-container-stack.
# Case 3938: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_container_stack_1mrslb(containers_opened: int = 7, containers_closed: int = 7, max_depth: int = 6, recovery_points: int = 2, stalled_positions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1mrslb = "1mrslb-blsrm1-3938"
    assert evidence_variant_1mrslb
    evidence_axes_1mrslb = (
        "reader1mrslb",
        "source3938",
        "parseblsrm1",
        "route1mrslb3938",
        "artifact39381mrslb",
        "recoveryblsrm13938",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1mrslb)) == 6
    if containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-count"
    if containers_closed != containers_opened:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-balance"
    if max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    if recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0:
        return False, stalled_positions, "recovery-progress"
    assert containers_closed == containers_opened
    return True, max_depth, "recoverable-nesting"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use an explicit open-container stack where depth, continuation, closure, and source ownership need controlled iteration.
